name: benchmark-kernel
description: Guide for benchmarking FlashInfer kernels with CUPTI timing
capabilities: Provides tutorial for CUPTI hardware-level GPU profiling
Includes command-line examples for FlashInfer benchmarks
Shows Python API usage with bench_gpu_time() function
Documents timing accuracy differences between CUPTI and CUDA events
Explains how to compare multiple backend implementations
author: flashinfer-ai
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing tutorial instructions for GPU kernel benchmarking. No executable code, network calls, or file system access. Static findings are false positives caused by the analyzer incorrectly flagging documentation strings and bash command examples as security vulnerabilities.
valueStatement: Accurate GPU kernel benchmarking is challenging due to measurement overhead. This skill provides step-by-step instructions for using CUPTI hardware profiling to get precise kernel execution times. Compare different backends like FlashAttention and cuDNN to find the fastest implementation for your workload.
userTitle: Benchmark GPU kernels with CUPTI timing

useCases.title: Measure kernel performance accurately
useCases.description: Use CUPTI hardware profiling to get precise GPU execution times without host overhead

useCases.title: Compare attention kernel backends
useCases.description: Benchmark different attention implementations to find the fastest for your workload

useCases.title: Profile FP8 GEMM operations
useCases.description: Measure performance of mixed-precision matrix multiplication kernels

promptTemplates.title: Basic kernel timing
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me benchmark my CUDA kernel using bench_gpu_time() with 30 iterations and 5 warmup runs

promptTemplates.title: Compare backends
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to benchmark BatchDecodeWithPagedKVCacheWrapper with fa2, cudnn, and cutlass backends

promptTemplates.title: Batch benchmarks
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a test list file to benchmark different batch sizes and sequence lengths

promptTemplates.title: Custom Python benchmarking
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a Python script that benchmarks my custom attention kernel with CUPTI timing

bestPractices: Install CUPTI for hardware-level accuracy when possible
Use reference checking to verify kernel correctness during benchmarking
Run sufficient iterations (30+) for statistical significance
antiPatterns: Using only CUDA events when CUPTI is available
Comparing backends without verifying output correctness

faq.question: Do I need CUPTI installed?
faq.answer: No, the framework automatically falls back to CUDA events if CUPTI is unavailable

faq.question: What is the minimum CUDA version?
faq.answer: CUPTI requires CUDA 13+, but CUDA events work with any CUDA version

faq.question: Can I benchmark non-FlashInfer kernels?
faq.answer: Yes, use bench_gpu_time() with any CUDA kernel function in your Python code

faq.question: Is my data safe during benchmarking?
faq.answer: Yes, benchmarking only measures execution time without accessing your input data

faq.question: Why are my benchmark results inconsistent?
faq.answer: Increase warmup and measurement iterations, and check for thermal throttling on your GPU

faq.question: How accurate is CUPTI compared to CUDA events?
faq.answer: CUPTI is more accurate for fast kernels under 50 microseconds, difference is negligible for longer kernels
